name: Ex-Government Agent
description: Former FBI, BATF, DEA, Secret Service, and similar federal law-enforcement agents who lost their careers after Dark Day and the NSB purge. Elite investigators with interrogation, clue-finding, and surveillance skills — not professional soldiers unless upgraded via related skills (e.g. SWAT-style builds). Many joined Spook Squad reluctantly after witnessing Hounds, Dopplegangers, and other supernatural threats.
packageNotes: Secondary skills use the same category access limits as O.C.C. Related Skills (no category bonuses unless noted on the related list).
SWAT-style combat builds: upgrade combat and weapon skills via related picks (p. 31).

finances.blackMarketAssets: 1D4*10000 in property (vehicles, home, furniture, computers, etc.)

miscellaneous: Three sets of government-issue dark suits

classAbilities.name: Federal Law-Enforcement Background
classAbilities.description: Covers FBI, BATF, DEA, Secret Service, and similar agencies — elite investigators with interrogation, clue-finding, and surveillance training. Criminal contacts and street knowledge aid evasion of NSB pursuit (pp. 30–31).

classAbilities.name: Investigation Focus (Not Soldier by Default)
classAbilities.description: Basic combat training only; most skills are investigative rather than military. SWAT-style builds: upgrade combat and weapon skills via O.C.C. Related Skills (p. 31).

classAbilities.name: Dark Day Fallout & NSB Hunted
classAbilities.description: Careers ended by Dark Day and the NSB purge; many operate underground as perceived traitors and criminals while evading a growing army of NSB operatives (pp. 30–31).

classAbilities.name: Spook Squad Volunteers
classAbilities.description: Often traumatized by loss of authority and open carry privileges; only the most dedicated or stubborn join Spook Squad after witnessing Hounds, Dopplegangers, or compromised superiors. Mix of well-intentioned patriots and self-righteous narrow-minded agents united against the conspiracy (pp. 30–31).

classAbilities.name: O.C.C. Bonuses
classAbilities.description: +1 on initiative and +1 on all perception rolls — `staticBonuses.combat` (p. 31).

name: Team Epsilon Trooper
description: Former U.S. special forces soldiers recruited into Spook Squad after discovering the supernatural truth behind Dark Day. Covers public units (Green Berets, SEALs, Marine Recon, Rangers, etc.) and the classified Team Epsilon inner circle. All undergo meditation and anti-supernatural targeting training at secret camps in the U.S. and Canada.
packageNotes: High M.E. is preferred (not required).
Language pick includes literacy in that language at the same +15% bonus.
Pick Public Special Forces or Team Epsilon (Inner Circle) at creation — bonuses differ; skill package is shared.
Secondary skills use the same category access limits as O.C.C. Related Skills (no category bonuses unless noted on the related list).

occSkillsCore.label: One language of choice (+15%, includes literacy)

categoryMinimums.label: At least two Physical skills required

classAbilities.name: Anti-Supernatural Training
classAbilities.description: Secret-camp meditation and targeting drills to resist mental domination and strike weak points on Hounds, Hunters, and other armored supernatural beings. Lore: Demon and Monster may be required for unfamiliar species (pp. 31–33).

classAbilities.name: Special Anti-Supernatural Bonuses
classAbilities.description: When the species is known, aimed attacks (ranged or hand to hand) bypass supernatural A.R. on a normal strike roll (target may parry/dodge); costs two melee actions. Damage applies to S.D.C./H.P.; worn body armor A.R. still applies (pp. 32–33).

specializations.name: Public Special Forces
specializations.description: Green Berets, SEALs, Marine Recon, Airborne Rangers, and similar public special forces units.

classAbilities.name: Alignment
classAbilities.description: Most are scrupulous or aberrant (ends-justify-means); lone wolves may be selfish; twisted principles may be miscreant or diabolic (p. 33).

specializations.name: Team Epsilon (Inner Circle)
specializations.description: Classified Team Epsilon soldiers with additional conditioning, skills, and mental hardening.

classAbilities.name: Team Epsilon Conditioning
classAbilities.description: Top-secret unit with additional training, physical conditioning, and skill breadth beyond public special forces (pp. 31–32).

finances.blackMarketAssets: 1D6×100 dollars per week Spook Squad stipend (varies week to week); mission funds and equipment per ADA assignment

armor: Improved full body armor (A.R. 15, 250 S.D.C.) when on military missions
miscellaneous: Basic survival kit (canteen, matches, survival knife, compass, MREs, insect repellent, flashlight)
Camo or black fatigues and combat boots
Civilian cover gear similar to Ex-Government Agent when traveling undercover

name: A.D.A. Field Agent
description: CIA and War Room field operatives — among the most highly trained espionage and assassination agents in Spook Squad. Recruits train in supernatural identification and countermeasures; most specialize in intelligence gathering, assassination, or dual training in both.
packageNotes: Choose Spy Specialist, Assassination Specialist, or Dual-Trained Agent at creation — specialty skills and bonuses differ.
Assassination Specialists require P.S. and P.E. 10 or higher.
Language pick includes literacy in that language at the same +15% bonus.
Secondary skills use the same category access limits as O.C.C. Related Skills (no category bonuses unless noted on the related list).

occSkillsCore.label: One language of choice (+15%, includes literacy)

finances.blackMarketAssets: 4D6×1000 in property (vehicles, clothing, housing, etc.); $3,000 monthly Spook Squad stipend

specializations.name: Spy Specialist
specializations.description: Information gathering and espionage — infiltration, intelligence, and covert operations.

occSkillsCore.label: One language of choice (+15%, includes literacy)

categoryMinimums.label: Spy specialists must select at least two Espionage skills

classAbilities.name: Spy Specialist Specialty Skills
classAbilities.description: Two Rogue skills of choice at +20% in addition to the shared ADA core package (p. 34).

classAbilities.name: Spy Specialist Bonuses
classAbilities.description: +2 perception, +1 pull punch, +1 roll with impact, +3 save vs mind control, +2 save vs horror factor — `staticBonuses` (p. 34).

specializations.name: Assassination Specialist
specializations.description: Silent death and termination — sniper training and combat-focused field work.

occSkillsCore.label: One language of choice (+15%, includes literacy)

classAbilities.name: Assassination Specialist Specialty Skills
classAbilities.description: Sniper and one W.P. of choice in addition to the shared ADA core package. Must upgrade Hand to Hand to Expert at creation via related skill (p. 34).

classAbilities.name: Assassination Specialist Bonuses
classAbilities.description: +2 initiative, +1 strike/parry/dodge, +3 pull punch, +4 damage, +4 save vs horror factor — `staticBonuses` (p. 34).

specializations.name: Dual-Trained Agent
specializations.description: Trained in both espionage and assassination — more flexible but less specialized than either single-focus agent.

occSkillsCore.label: One language of choice (+15%, includes literacy)

classAbilities.name: Dual-Trained Specialty Skills
classAbilities.description: One Rogue skill of choice at +15% and one W.P. of choice in addition to the shared ADA core package (p. 34).

classAbilities.name: Dual-Trained Bonuses
classAbilities.description: +1 perception, +1 parry/dodge, +1 pull punch, +1 roll with impact, +2 save vs mind control, +3 save vs horror factor — `staticBonuses` (p. 34).

classAbilities.name: CIA / War Room Operative
classAbilities.description: Elite HUMINT survivors from a shrinking CIA and the larger War Room reservoir — trained in espionage and assassination, now focused on identifying and combating supernatural threats (pp. 33–34).

classAbilities.name: Supernatural Identification Training
classAbilities.description: All ADA field agents receive extensive training in recognizing supernatural creatures and countermeasures beyond standard espionage tradecraft (p. 34).

name: Pandora Project Researcher
description: Elite PAB and Pandora Project scientists — multi-skilled researchers with parapsychology and occult expertise, among the leading human authorities on the supernatural invasion. Most work in Spook Squad R&D; field researchers receive combat training for firsthand enemy study.
packageNotes: +1 initiative applies only when fighting supernatural or mystical opponents (p. 36).
Hand to Hand must be purchased from O.C.C. Related Skills — not included in the core package.
Select at least four related skills from Medical, Science, or Technical (or prerequisites to such skills).
Language pick includes literacy in that language at the same +25% bonus.
Secondary skills use the same category access limits as O.C.C. Related Skills (no category bonuses unless noted on the related list).

occSkillsCore.label: Language and literacy (one language of choice, +25%)
Two Medical, Science, or Technical skills of choice

finances.blackMarketAssets: 5D6×1000 in personal property; $4,000 monthly Spook Squad stipend

miscellaneous: Research facility access with $50,000 in lab equipment
weapons: Handgun or small sub-machinegun for self-defense (if character has a W.P.)

classAbilities.name: Recognize the Supernatural
classAbilities.description: In-depth supernatural recognition beyond lore skills — mind control/possession, Nightbane Morphus, vampires, magic circles/symbols/portals; Dopplegangers and Ashmedai at half skill. One roll per subject; failure means uncertainty (p. 36).

classAbilities.name: R&D and Field Research
classAbilities.description: Leading human authorities on the invasion via PAB access; many work Spook Squad R&D while a few field researchers accept combat training. Prejudices can obscure facts (pp. 35–36).

classAbilities.name: O.C.C. Bonuses
classAbilities.description: +2 perception; +1 initiative only vs supernatural/mystical opponents; +1 save vs magic and psionics; +2 save vs horror factor — `staticBonuses` and `packageNotes` (p. 36).

classAbilities.name: Scholar Background
classAbilities.description: Multi-skilled scientists spanning medicine, physics, chemistry, parapsychology, and occultism — ideal recruits combine broad knowledge with one or two specialties (pp. 35–36).

name: P.A.B. Field Agent
description: Paranormal Activities Bureau special agents recruited from the FBI, DEA, and NSA — veterans of paranormal encounters trained to investigate and combat supernatural beings, psychics, magic, and UFO phenomena. After Dark Day, most PAB survivors joined Spook Squad as dedicated field operatives.
packageNotes: Secondary skills use the same category access limits as O.C.C. Related Skills (no category bonuses unless noted on the related list).
Lore: Demon and Monster is a prerequisite for Special Anti-Supernatural aimed attacks against unfamiliar creatures (Pass B).

occSkillsCore.label: W.P. any modern weapon of choice

finances.blackMarketAssets: 4D6×1000 in property (vehicles, clothing, housing, etc.); $3,600 monthly Spook Squad stipend

weapons: Small sub-machinegun (usually carried in a briefcase)
miscellaneous: Psionic detection system, psychic jammer, and special restraints (typical two-agent team)
NSB/Special Forces-grade weapons and body armor on emergency or combat missions

classAbilities.name: Special Anti-Supernatural Bonuses
classAbilities.description: When the species is known (Lore: Demon and Monster may be required for unfamiliar creatures), aimed attacks bypass supernatural A.R. on a normal strike roll (target may parry/dodge); costs two melee actions. Damage applies to S.D.C./H.P.; worn body armor A.R. still applies (pp. 35–36).

classAbilities.name: Paranormal Field Experience
classAbilities.description: Most agents have confronted supernatural beings, psychics, and unfathomable mysteries; survivors of Dark Day bring hard-earned PAB knowledge into Spook Squad (pp. 34–35).

classAbilities.name: O.C.C. Bonuses
classAbilities.description: +2 perception, +1 initiative, +3 save vs psionics/mind control/possession, +2 save vs magic, +4 save vs horror factor — `staticBonuses` (p. 35).

name: P.A.B. Psychic Agent
description: Pandora Project field operatives with awakened psionic abilities — among the few humans who can sense the supernatural, expose Dopplegangers, and pierce the Nightlords' veil. Survivors of the Dark Day purge now serve Spook Squad; additional latent psychics volunteer as the war continues.
packageNotes: Psionic picks at 1st level: three powers from Sensitive, Physical, or Healing only (lesser categories).
Secondary skills use the same category access limits as O.C.C. Related Skills (no category bonuses unless noted on the related list).

finances.blackMarketAssets: 2D4×10000 in personal property; 1D4×1000+2000 monthly Spook Squad stipend

weapons: 9mm pistol with conventional and silver bullets
Additional rifles, explosives, and PAB special equipment on assignment

classAbilities.name: Psychic Power Program
classAbilities.description: Select three powers from Sensitive, Physical, or Healing at 1st level; gain one additional power from those categories at levels 3, 5, 9, and 12 — `ispEngine.progressionRoadmap` (pp. 36–37).

classAbilities.name: I.S.P.
classAbilities.description: M.E. + 5D6 I.S.P. at 1st level; +2D4 I.S.P. per additional level — `ispEngine` (p. 36).

classAbilities.name: Physical Training
classAbilities.description: Intensive conditioning: +2D6 S.D.C., +1 P.S., +1D6 Spd. (plus physical skill bonuses) — `staticBonuses` (p. 36).

classAbilities.name: Combat Bonuses
classAbilities.description: +1 initiative, +1 parry/dodge, +1 roll with impact, +1 save vs psionic attack and mind control, +3 save vs horror factor — `staticBonuses` (p. 36).

classAbilities.name: Pandora Project Origins
classAbilities.description: Latent psychics recruited and awakened through Pandora Project research; ~30 of ~100 pre–Dark Day field agents survived Ba'al pursuit. PAB can endow psionic power via Pandora Techniques, but difficulty and consequences limit wider use (pp. 36–37).

classAbilities.name: Ba'al Fear of Psychics
classAbilities.description: Psychics can sense the supernatural, expose Dopplegangers, and recognize the scale of the government takeover — making them priority targets for Nightlord minions (p. 36).

name: Mind Master P.C.C.
description: Pre–Dark Day elite psychics who lived in the shadows — wealthy, reclusive, or quietly helpful until the Nightlords' invasion exposed and targeted them. Among the first to sense the Ba'al takeover; many now warn other psychics, join Spook Squad or resistance factions, or fight as vigilantes. A tiny fraction serve the Nightlords.
packageNotes: Must be human (p. 119).
Master psionic saving throw class (p. 119).
Autostart psionics at 1st level: Alter Aura, Detect Psionics, Empathy, Mind Block, Object Read, Presence Sense, See Aura, See the Invisible, Telepathy, Total Recall (p. 119).

Bonus 1st-level picks: select 2 Super/Master psionics OR 3 Sensitive psionics — mutually exclusive; player completes one branch only (same UX as Psychic Gate Major: 8-from-one-category vs 6-mixed) (p. 119).
Each level after 1st: select one power from Super, Sensitive, or Healing (p. 119).
I.S.P.: 2D6×10 + M.E. at 1st level; +3D4 per additional level (p. 119).
P.C.C. bonuses: +1 save vs psionics and mind control (incl. drugs), +1 perception, +3 possession/insanity, +3 dream manipulation/combat, +4 horror factor (p. 119).
Physical: any except Acrobatics and Gymnastics (p. 119).
Hand to Hand must be purchased as a Related skill (Basic = 1 slot, Expert = 2, Martial Arts or Assassin = 3; Assassin evil only) (p. 119).
Secondary skills: five at creation — no O.C.C. % bonus; same category limits as Related Skills (p. 119).
Wealthier pre-invasion Mind Masters were prime Nightlord targets — typical gear reflects modest survivors (p. 119).

occSkillsCore.label: Two Technical or Science skills of choice

creationSelectionPlan.label: Bonus psionics — branch A: 2 Super/Master (complete this branch OR branch B, not both)
Bonus psionics — branch B: 3 Sensitive (complete this branch OR branch A, not both)

miscellaneous: Modest wardrobe and basic personal items

classAbilities.name: Supernatural Senses
classAbilities.description: Sense supernatural beings within 60 feet (18.3 m) +5 feet (1.5 m) per level — no I.S.P. cost; alerts when a supernatural creature enters or leaves range (does not pinpoint location). When observing an individual supernatural creature, detect its true nature at 30% +5% per level; Alter Aura and Nightbane Facade reduce this chance by 30% (p. 119).

classAbilities.name: Master Psionic Program
classAbilities.description: Ten autostart psionics at 1st level plus exclusive bonus branch (2 Super or 3 Sensitive) and one Super/Sensitive/Healing pick per level thereafter — see `ispEngine` (p. 119).

classAbilities.name: Inner Strength Points (I.S.P.)
classAbilities.description: 2D6×10 + M.E. at 1st level; +3D4 I.S.P. per additional level. Master psionic saving throw class (p. 119).

classAbilities.name: P.C.C. Bonuses
classAbilities.description: +1 save vs psionics and mind control (including drugs), +1 perception, +3 possession/insanity, +3 dream manipulation/combat, +4 horror factor — encoded in `staticBonuses` (p. 119).

classAbilities.name: Pre–Dark Day Shadow Elite
classAbilities.description: Wealthy or reclusive psychics who avoided the limelight and evaded PAB detection; "live and let live" credo shattered by the Nightlords' invasion (pp. 119–120).

classAbilities.name: Nightlord Targeting & Resistance
classAbilities.description: Ba'al minions ferreted out hundreds of Mind Masters before psychics realized they were marked for extermination. Many now warn other psychics and Nightbane, join Spook Squad, PAB remnants, militias, or Nightbane factions; a tiny fraction serve the Nightlords (pp. 119–120).

classAbilities.name: Psychic First Line of Defense
classAbilities.description: Psychics sense Nightlord minions in human guise, detect astral spies, and counter supernatural threats on Earth and the Astral Plane — among the few who pierce the Ba'al veil (pp. 119–120).

name: 'Kinetic P.C.C.
description: Teens and young adults whose psychokinetic gifts erupted violently — many were hunted, imprisoned, or killed by the PAB and Pandora Project before Dark Day. The invasion awakened thousands more. Spook Squad recruitment forces a bitter choice between former captors and fighting the Ba'al alone; many join, while others band with psychics, Nightbane factions, militias, or operate solo.
packageNotes: Major psychokinetic power: select one specialization at creation — Electrokinesis, Hydrokinesis, Pyrokinesis, or Telekinesis (pp. 120–121).
Other psionics: three Physical powers at 1st level; one Physical or Healing power per additional level (p. 121).
I.S.P.: 2D4×10 + 10 + M.E. at 1st level; +2D6 per additional level (p. 121).
P.C.C. bonuses: +1 save vs psionics, +2 possession/insanity, +1 dream manipulation/combat, +2 horror factor (p. 121).
Background-driven skill picks — GM approves unusual requests (p. 121). Most discover powers at ages 15–17.
Physical: any except Acrobatics and Gymnastics (p. 122).
Hand to Hand must be purchased as a Related skill (Basic = 1 slot, Expert = 2, Martial Arts or Assassin = 3; Assassin evil only) (p. 121).
Secondary skills: five at creation — no O.C.C. % bonus; same category limits as Related Skills (p. 122).
Spook Squad recruits use Spook Squad agent/researcher equipment benefits (p. 122).

creationSelectionPlan.label: 1st level Physical psionics (in addition to major power specialization)

perLevelSelection.label: Additional Physical or Healing psionic per level

classAbilities.name: Major Psychokinetic Power
classAbilities.description: Select one specialization at creation — Electrokinesis, Hydrokinesis, Pyrokinesis, or Telekinesis. That power is used better than standard catalog values; see active specialization `classAbilities` (pp. 120–121).

classAbilities.name: Other Psionic Powers
classAbilities.description: Three Physical psionics at 1st level (in addition to major power); one Physical or Healing psionic per additional level — see `ispEngine` (p. 121).

classAbilities.name: Inner Strength Points (I.S.P.)
classAbilities.description: 2D4×10 + 10 + M.E. at 1st level; +2D6 I.S.P. per additional level. Major psionic saving throw class (p. 121).

classAbilities.name: P.C.C. Bonuses
classAbilities.description: +1 save vs psionics, +2 possession/insanity, +1 dream manipulation/combat, +2 horror factor — encoded in `staticBonuses` (p. 121).

classAbilities.name: PAB & Pandora Project Persecution
classAbilities.description: Pre–Dark Day PAB documented ~50 violent psychokinetic incidents — mostly teens — with cover-ups blaming gas explosions and storms; many subjects killed or imprisoned by PAB/Pandora Project (p. 120).

classAbilities.name: Nightlord Extermination & Spook Squad
classAbilities.description: Dark Day awakened thousands; Ba'al targeted psychics but 'Kinetics proved costly to hunt. Spook Squad recruitment forces alliance with former captors or fighting alone — many join factions, gangs, militias, or operate solo (pp. 120–121).

classAbilities.name: Teen Awakening Background
classAbilities.description: Most discover powers at ages 15–17 during emotional outbursts; half never finish high school — skill picks should reflect background; GM approves unusual skills (pp. 121–122).

specializations.name: Electrokinesis
specializations.description: Major psychokinetic power: Electrokinesis — enhanced electrical arcs, device manipulation range, and electricity sense (Nightbane RPG p. 79; BTS p. 120).

classAbilities.name: Major Power: Electrokinesis
classAbilities.description: Base catalog power `psionic_electrokinesis` plus all standard electrokinesis sub-abilities from Nightbane RPG p. 79 (p. 120).

classAbilities.name: Electrical Arc Attack
classAbilities.description: Generate electrical arcs inflicting 5D6 S.D.C. damage. Range: 50 feet (15.2 m) + 10 feet (3 m) per level of experience. I.S.P. cost: 15 (p. 120).

classAbilities.name: Enhanced Manipulate & Sense Electricity
classAbilities.description: Base range to manipulate electrical devices or sense electricity increased to 60 feet (18.3 m) + 5 feet (1.5 m) per level of experience (p. 120).

specializations.name: Hydrokinesis
specializations.description: Major psychokinetic power: Hydrokinesis — water pressure bursts, high-pressure jets, and sense water (Nightbane RPG p. 79; BTS p. 120).

classAbilities.name: Major Power: Hydrokinesis
classAbilities.description: Base catalog power `psionic_hydrokinesis` plus all standard hydrokinesis sub-abilities from Nightbane RPG p. 79 (p. 120).

classAbilities.name: Water Volume & Pressure Manipulation
classAbilities.description: Manipulate up to 50 gallons of water + 10 gallons per level; by manipulating pressure can burst pipes or fire hydrants (p. 120).

classAbilities.name: High-Pressure Water Jet
classAbilities.description: Channel a high-pressure jet inflicting 1D6 damage per 20 gallons used. Range: 20 feet (6.1 m) + 5 feet (1.5 m) per level of experience (p. 120).

classAbilities.name: Enhanced Sense Water
classAbilities.description: Sense water range: 60 feet (18.3 m) + 5 feet (1.5 m) per level of experience (p. 120).

specializations.name: Pyrokinesis
specializations.description: Major psychokinetic power: Pyrokinesis — discounted fireballs and doubled base ranges (Nightbane RPG p. 81; BTS p. 120).

classAbilities.name: Major Power: Pyrokinesis
classAbilities.description: Base catalog power `psionic_pyrokinesis` plus all standard pyrokinesis sub-abilities from Nightbane RPG p. 81 (p. 120).

classAbilities.name: Discount Fireball
classAbilities.description: Fireballs cost 15 I.S.P. (reduced from standard) with range 60 feet (18.3 m) + 5 feet (1.5 m) per level of experience (p. 120).

classAbilities.name: Doubled Base Ranges
classAbilities.description: Base range of all other pyrokinesis powers is doubled; per-level range increment remains the same as standard catalog values (p. 120).

specializations.name: Telekinesis
specializations.description: Major psychokinetic power: Telekinesis — 10 lbs per I.S.P. and extended throw/manipulation range (Nightbane RPG p. 82; BTS p. 121).

classAbilities.name: Major Power: Telekinesis
classAbilities.description: Base catalog power `psionic_telekinesis` plus all standard telekinetic sub-abilities from Nightbane RPG p. 82 (p. 121).

classAbilities.name: Enhanced Lift Capacity
classAbilities.description: Weight manipulated becomes 10 lbs (4.5 kg) per I.S.P. spent (improved from standard telekinesis scaling) (p. 121).

classAbilities.name: Enhanced Throw & Manipulation Range
classAbilities.description: Throwing and manipulation range increased to 30 feet (9.1 m) + 10 feet (3 m) per level of experience, regardless of weight lifted (p. 121).

miscellaneous: 1D4×1,000 dollars in possessions (clothes, weapons, personal items)

finances.blackMarketAssets: 1D4*1000 in possessions; or one month's salary in savings if higher (p. 122)

name: Natural & Genius P.C.C.
description: Latent psychics who unconsciously channel energy into mundane excellence — Olympic-tier athletes, master hackers, and effortless savants overlooked in the psychic arms race. Many now serve Spook Squad, the Seekers, or corporations; their gifts make them quicker to spot plots, subterfuge, and the supernatural.
packageNotes: No attribute requirements — all attributes +1 at creation (3D6+1 each; roll extra 1D6 if total 16+ before bonuses) (p. 123).
Subconscious psionics: Mind Block and Total Recall (p. 122).
I.S.P.: M.E. + 3D6 at 1st level; +1D4 per additional level (p. 122).
P.P.E.: 4D6+16 + P.E. at 1st level — not increased per level; minimum 2 P.P.E. permanent base after specialty spending (p. 122).
At creation spend initial P.P.E. on permanent +3% skill boosts and/or P.P.E. specialties — see `classAbilities` (pp. 122–123).
Hand to Hand must be purchased as a Related skill (Basic = 1 slot, Expert = 2, Martial Arts or Assassin = 3; Assassin evil only) (p. 123).
Spook Squad option: exchange one Technical or one Electrical core skill for W.P. Pistol and Hand to Hand: Basic (p. 123).
Secondary skills: three at 1st level, plus three more at levels 3, 6, 10, and 14 (p. 124).
Open related-skill access — GM approves unlikely skills by background (p. 124).

occSkillsCore.label: Two Technical or Science skills of choice
Two Electrical, Mechanical, or Medical skills of choice

classAbilities.name: Subconscious Psionics
classAbilities.description: Mind Block and Total Recall — subconscious powers used when needed; many naturals are unaware they are psychics. Encoded on `ispEngine.grantedAbilityIds` (pp. 122–123).

classAbilities.name: Inner Strength Points (I.S.P.)
classAbilities.description: M.E. + 3D6 at 1st level; +1D4 I.S.P. per additional level. Minor psionic saving throw class (p. 122).

classAbilities.name: Attribute Bonuses at Creation
classAbilities.description: +1 to all attributes (roll 3D6+1 each; if total including bonuses is 16+, roll an additional 1D6). No minimum attribute requirements (p. 122).

classAbilities.name: P.P.E. Pool & Minimum Reserve
classAbilities.description: 4D6+16 + P.E. at 1st level — no per-level P.P.E. gain. Much of this pool is unconsciously spent on specialties at creation; retain minimum 2 P.P.E. as permanent base. P.E. gained from a specialty does not count toward this initial P.P.E. total (p. 122).

classAbilities.name: Permanent Skill Boost (P.P.E. Option)
classAbilities.description: Alternatively to specialties: spend permanent P.P.E. to grant +3% to any one skill per point spent. Bonus is permanent and cannot be changed or transferred (p. 122).

classAbilities.name: P.P.E. Specialty Program
classAbilities.description: Spend set P.P.E. costs at creation on one or more specialties below; any combination allowed, limited only by available P.P.E. (after minimum 2 retained). Specialties stack with formal skills and O.C.C. bonuses (pp. 122–123).

classAbilities.name: Specialty: Electrical Genius (16 P.P.E.)
classAbilities.description: Intuitive electronics sense — Basic Electronics at 65% +5% per level (not equivalent to full engineering training). One-time +15% to all Electrical skills learned through formal education; half penalties on extremely advanced or alien technology. Electrical Hot-wiring 66% +2% per level (most systems; GM may apply −5% to −25% under stress, speed, or alien complexity) (pp. 122–123).

classAbilities.name: Specialty: Computer Genius (15 P.P.E.)
classAbilities.description: Computer Operation 88% +2%/level, Computer Programming 80% +2%/level, Computer Hacking 70% +2%/level. Learn unfamiliar/cutting-edge systems at 56% +4%/level. +2 on perception rolls (p. 123).

classAbilities.name: Specialty: Mental Genius (17 P.P.E.)
classAbilities.description: +1D4+2 to I.Q. (minimum 17), photographic memory, +3% bonus on all skills including secondary (cumulative with I.Q. and O.C.C. bonuses). +2 perception, +2 save vs dream manipulation/combat, +2 to see through illusions (p. 123).

classAbilities.name: Specialty: Weapons Expert (16 P.P.E.)
classAbilities.description: Three bonus Weapon Proficiencies beyond O.C.C. picks. Make/modify any weapon with a W.P. at 72% +2%/level (materials/tools required; GM penalties −5% to −65% by complexity). Maintain weapons and recognize quality at 50% +5%/level. +1 parry with ancient weapons and +1 strike with ranged weapons (W.P. only). Select one favorite weapon (specific make/model) for +2 strike and +2 parry when applicable (pp. 123–124).

classAbilities.name: Specialty: Bio-Feedback — Death Trance (20 P.P.E.)
classAbilities.description: Death Trance cancels drug/poison effects temporarily; maintain 24 hours per level while antidote sought or self-cleansing attempted. Base 88% +2% per level (p. 123).

classAbilities.name: Specialty: Bio-Feedback — Cleansing Trance
classAbilities.description: While in trance, 24-hour meditative cleansing at 66% +2%/level restores 1D6 S.D.C. or H.P. per hour and flushes/negates poisons, drugs, and toxins (p. 123).

classAbilities.name: Specialty: Bio-Feedback — Positive Chi Channel
classAbilities.description: Channel positive energy at 70% +2%/level: energy attacks (heat, fire, electricity, lasers) and gases/poisons/drugs do half damage/duration; +1 save vs magic, +2 vs psionics, +4 vs possession, +4 vs dream manipulation/combat, +5 vs horror factor. Benefits last 20 minutes per hour of prior positive-chi meditation (p. 123).

classAbilities.name: Specialty: Exceptional Physical Prowess (18 P.P.E.)
classAbilities.description: +1D6 P.P. (minimum 17), +1D6+6 Spd (minimum 17), +1 attack per melee, +3 initiative, +3 roll with punch/fall/impact, +3 pull punch, and one-time +10% to a favorite physical skill (prowl, climbing, swimming, gymnastics, or similar) (p. 123).

classAbilities.name: Specialty: Exceptional Physical Strength (18 P.P.E.)
classAbilities.description: +1D6+6 P.S. (minimum 18), +1D4 P.E., +1D4×10 S.D.C. Punches and kicks inflict supernatural P.S. damage per Nightbane RPG p. 35 (p. 123).

classAbilities.name: Specialty: Exceptional Physical Endurance (18 P.P.E.)
classAbilities.description: +2D4 P.E. (minimum 18), +1D6×10 S.D.C., +2D6 H.P., +10% survive coma/death, +2 save vs disease, poisons, and drugs — cumulative with P.E. bonuses (p. 123).

classAbilities.name: Overlooked Savants & Supernatural Awareness
classAbilities.description: Latent psychics ignored by both sides of the war until necessity forces choices — dozens serve Spook Squad, Seekers, or corporations; gifts make them quicker to recognize the unusual, plots, and subterfuge (pp. 122–124).

classAbilities.name: Spook Squad Skill Exchange
classAbilities.description: Characters working for Spook Squad or similar may exchange one Technical or one Electrical core skill for W.P. Pistol and Hand to Hand: Basic (p. 123).

classAbilities.name: Flexible Skill Learning
classAbilities.description: May learn almost any skill of interest with I.Q. and O.C.C. bonuses; GM approves unlikely skills by background, age, and history — naturals may "bend the rules" via book learning (p. 124).

miscellaneous: 6D6×1,000 dollars in possessions (nice car/motorcycle, apartment, clothing)

name: Psi-Mechanic P.C.C.
description: Psychics who imprint powers into pseudo-scientific gadgets — machines useless to most people unless another psychic channels I.S.P. through them. A centerpiece of Pandora Project research at Detroit Metallurgical Laboratories until Dark Day scattered survivors and their impossible devices.
packageNotes: Device powers: six picks at 1st level — two each from Physical, Sensitive, and Healing; usable only through self-built psi-devices (pp. 124–125).
Independent psionics (no device): Telemechanics, Total Recall, Object Read (p. 125).

Per-level device powers (levels 2–15): branch A — 2 Physical/Sensitive/Healing per level OR branch B — 1 Super per level; complete one branch per level only (same UX as Mind Master ruling-01) (p. 125).
P.P.E.: 6D6+20 + P.E. at 1st level; minimum 8 retained; remainder builds devices; +2D6 P.P.E./level for more gadgets (p. 125).
I.S.P.: 2D6×10 + M.E. at 1st level; +10 per additional level (p. 125).
Physical: any except Acrobatics (p. 125).
Hand to Hand must be purchased as a Related skill (Basic = 1 slot, Expert = 2, Martial Arts or Assassin = 3; Assassin evil only) (p. 125).
Secondary skills: six at creation — no O.C.C. % bonus; same category limits as Related Skills (p. 125).
Starting gear includes psi-devices equal to invested P.P.E. plus workshop tools (p. 125).

occSkillsCore.label: Three Mechanical, Electrical, or Technical skills of choice
Two Science or Domestic skills of choice

creationSelectionPlan.label: Per-level device powers (levels 2–15) — branch B: 1 Super/Master each level (complete branch B OR branch A each level, not both)

perLevelSelection.label: Per-level device powers — branch A: 2 Physical/Sensitive/Healing each level (complete branch A OR branch B each level, not both)

classAbilities.name: Psychological Device Dependency
classAbilities.description: Without self-built machines and gadgets, the Psi-Mechanic usually cannot tap device-imprinted psionic powers — a psychological block, but no less real. Gizmos are useless to most non-psychics (pp. 124–125).

classAbilities.name: Build Psionic Devices (Imprint)
classAbilities.description: Build a device emulating one known psionic power per gadget. Imprint drains P.P.E. when built: spend 1 P.P.E. per 2 I.S.P. of the power's activation cost, or 4 P.P.E. minimum — whichever is more. Must already know the power to imprint. Devices follow pseudo-scientific common sense (goggles/binoculars for see invisible, helmets for telepathy, motion detectors for sensing, etc.); may be real gadgets that also function normally (pp. 124–125).

classAbilities.name: Device Activation
classAbilities.description: Devices draw I.S.P. from the user at the same cost and potency as the standard catalog psionic power they emulate (p. 125).

classAbilities.name: Operating Another Psi-Mechanic's Device
classAbilities.description: Psi-Mechanic: 20% base chance to use a found device without creator instructions. Other psychics: 12%. Under careful instruction from the creator, any psychic learns at 55%. Object Read adds +5%; Telemechanics adds +10% (+15% if both used) (p. 125).

classAbilities.name: P.P.E. for Device Construction
classAbilities.description: 6D6+20 + P.E. at 1st level; minimum 8 P.P.E. retained — remainder may imprint devices. +2D6 P.P.E. per additional level for more gadgets. See `ppeEngine` (p. 125).

classAbilities.name: Device-Only Psionic Program
classAbilities.description: Six device powers at 1st level (two each from Physical, Sensitive, Healing). Each level after 1st: branch A — two picks from those categories, OR branch B — one Super/Master pick (exclusive per level). All device picks require an imprinted gadget to use — see `ispEngine` (pp. 124–125).

classAbilities.name: Independent Psionics (No Device)
classAbilities.description: Telemechanics, Total Recall, and Object Read function without machines — encoded on `ispEngine.grantedAbilityIds` (p. 125).

classAbilities.name: Inner Strength Points (I.S.P.)
classAbilities.description: 2D6×10 + M.E. at 1st level; +10 I.S.P. per additional level. Major psionic saving throw class (p. 125).

classAbilities.name: Pandora Project / DML Research
classAbilities.description: Major study subject at Detroit Metallurgical Laboratories — theories: (1) the Psi-Mechanic's own abilities power the gadget, and (2) psychic imprint acts as trigger and circuit board. Science could not explain why devices work for creators; many lack special mechanical components and some do not function as ordinary machines (pp. 124–125).

classAbilities.name: Imprint Uniqueness
classAbilities.description: Another Psi-Mechanic cannot usually operate someone else's machine without instructions — the imprint is creator-specific (p. 124).

classAbilities.name: Mechanical Aptitude vs. Psychic-Only Tinkerer
classAbilities.description: Most develop mechanical-science aptitude; a few are clumsy with "real" machines and build only psychic devices unrelated to physical laws — player chooses at creation and adjusts skill picks accordingly (p. 125).

miscellaneous: Psi-devices and gadgets equal to P.P.E. invested at creation
Personal computer and printer, laptop, calculator, pen flashlight
Pocket tool kit, full tool kit, duffle bag, backpack
Modest wardrobe, old automobile, modest rented apartment

finances.blackMarketAssets: Most cash spent on tools, materials, and gizmo construction (p. 125)

name: Arcane Detective O.C.C.
description: Mystically trained investigators — often tied to the Aurora Investigation Agency or the Seekers — who blend detective tradecraft with investigative spells. Fewer than a hundred existed before the war; Spook Squad and allied groups now train more as supernatural crime and Nightlord plots draw them into the resistance.
packageNotes: Initial spells: See Aura, See the Invisible, Sense Evil, Sense Magic, Detect Concealment, Astral Projection — plus 1D4+2 more from levels 1–4 (9–12 total) (p. 126).
Each additional level: one new spell equal to character level (p. 126).
Pursuit of Magic: may learn or purchase spells and rituals of any level regardless of experience (Nightbane RPG; p. 126).
P.P.E.: 1D4×10 + P.E. at 1st level; +2D6 per additional level (p. 126).
Bonuses: +1 initiative, +2 perception, +4 horror, +2 possession, +1 dream manipulation/combat; +1 save vs magic at 3/6/9/12; +1 spell strength at 4/8/12 (p. 127).
Lore core skill: Demon and Monster OR Geomancy (player choice) (p. 127).
Physical related: any except Acrobatics; +5% to Prowl and Climbing when selected (p. 127).
Hand to Hand: Basic included; upgrade to Expert costs 1 related slot, Martial Arts or Assassin costs 2 (p. 127).
Secondary skills: five at creation — no O.C.C. % bonus; same category limits as Related Skills (p. 127).
Informant network built during play with GM (p. 126).

occSkillsCore.label: Lore: Demon and Monster OR Geomancy

classAbilities.name: Investigation & Magic Synergy
classAbilities.description: Combination of detective tradecraft and investigative spells makes Arcane Detectives ultimate truth-seekers — locate missing persons, uncover supernatural activity, and solve crimes beyond mundane methods (p. 126).

classAbilities.name: Initial Spell Knowledge
classAbilities.description: At 1st level: See Aura, See the Invisible, Sense Evil, Sense Magic, Detect Concealment, and Astral Projection — plus 1D4+2 additional wizard spells from levels 1–4 (9–12 total). See `ppeEngine.grantedAbilityIds` and level-1 `progressionRoadmap` (p. 126).

classAbilities.name: Spell Advancement
classAbilities.description: Each additional level, select one new spell equal to character level — see `ppeEngine.progressionRoadmap` levels 2–15 (p. 126).

classAbilities.name: Learning New Spells (Pursuit of Magic)
classAbilities.description: Additional spells and rituals of any magic level may be learned or purchased at any time regardless of experience level — `ppeEngine.spellAcquisition: open` (Nightbane RPG; p. 126).

classAbilities.name: P.P.E. Reservoir
classAbilities.description: 1D4×10 + P.E. at 1st level; +2D6 P.P.E. per additional level — see `ppeEngine` (pp. 126–127).

classAbilities.name: Detective Bonuses
classAbilities.description: +1 initiative, +2 perception, +4 horror factor, +2 possession, +1 dream manipulation/combat; +1 save vs magic at levels 3, 6, 9, and 12; +1 spell strength at levels 4, 8, and 12 — encoded in `staticBonuses`, `levelGatedSaves`, and `ppeEngine.spellStrengthProgression` (p. 127).

classAbilities.name: Law Enforcement Connections
classAbilities.description: Many detectives maintain ties to local authorities — friends, acquaintances, and paid informants on the police force who provide leads, confirm facts or rumors, and occasional assistance (p. 126).

classAbilities.name: Street Informant Network
classAbilities.description: Alternatively (or additionally), a web of street connections: bartenders, street people, stool-pigeons, fellow detectives, reporters (or their cleaning staff), and petty criminals who share information for a gratuity — payoff, reward cut, favor, bottle of booze, etc. GM and player build this network over the campaign; role-play heavily (p. 126).

classAbilities.name: Paranormal Crime Specialty
classAbilities.description: Specializes in supernatural and paranormal crimes — covens, cultists, sorcerers, vampires, and other monstrous predators; no stranger to cases mundane police cannot touch (p. 126).

classAbilities.name: Nightlord Exposure
classAbilities.description: Many stumble onto Ba'al plots and pay dearly; some devote more time to opposing the Nightlords and recruiting allies, while others steer clear of Ba'al minions and pawns (p. 126).

classAbilities.name: Aurora Investigation Agency & Seekers
classAbilities.description: Many belong to this rare fraternity — often associated with the Aurora Investigation Agency (Organizations of the Astral Plane) or the Seekers faction (Nightbane RPG). Fewer than a hundred believed to exist before the war (p. 126).

classAbilities.name: Growing Ranks & Spook Squad Training
classAbilities.description: Spook Squad and allied organizations are discovering and training more mystic investigators as the supernatural war expands (p. 126).

miscellaneous: Pocket tape recorder, 35 mm camera with telephoto lens
Small office in a run-down building

name: Millek R.C.C. Skills
description: Shadow O.C.C. for `race_millek` — astral-native psychic guides (pp. 64–66). Fixed R.C.C. skill package plus 10 related and 6 secondary skills. Hand to Hand: Martial Arts (included). Eight Sensitive psionics at 1st level; one additional power from any category (including master) per level after 1st. I.S.P. M.E.+6D6, +3D6/level. P.P.E. 1D6×10+P.E., +2D4/level on race vitals. Cannot learn magic.
packageNotes: Shadow O.C.C. for `race_millek` (auto-mounted via forcedOccId). Optional astral R.C.C. — young PCs typically 1st–2nd level; sages 4–7 (1D4+3), old sages 1D6+9 (pp. 64–66).
Alignment: any good or selfish; evil Milleks almost unheard of (pp. 64–65).
Cannot materialize on Earth/Nightlands — astral traveler only unless the character has astral projection/transference (pp. 65–66).
P.P.E. dependency and astral-domain / staff / combat natural abilities live on `race_millek` — see `classAbilities` cross-refs (pp. 64–66).

Fixed R.C.C. skills in `occSkillsCore` (native Millek 98% via `skill_language` basePercent); Rogue picks non-technological only; archaic W.P. only (`wpRules.coreWps`: W.P. Staff); +1 related skill at levels 3, 5, 8, and 11 (`levelUpSkillChoices`) (pp. 65–66).
Psionics: eight Sensitive picks at 1st; +1 from any category (including master) each level after 1st — `ispEngine` (pp. 64–65).
Experience: `between_shadows_millek_astral_mage` XP table (p. 145).

leyLine.notes: Innate psionics inherit psionic_global_rules.json ley-line/nexus multipliers when applicable.

psychicApm.notes: Combat as Hand to Hand: Martial Arts; psionic attacks per melee equal physical attacks when psionic powers are active.

creationSelectionPlan.label: Eight Sensitive psionic powers at 1st level

perLevelSelection.label: One psionic from any category (including master) per level

miscellaneous: Psionically attuned staff (2D6 damage to most astral beings; double vs entities and Necrophim)
Bag with scrolls, maps, and knowledge sources

classAbilities.name: Fixed R.C.C. Skill Package
classAbilities.description: Millek (98%), three languages (+20%), Acrobatics (+10%), Detect Ambush (+10%), Lore: Psychic (+15%), Lore: Astral (+25%), Prowl (+10%), Lore: Demon & Monster (+15%), W.P. Staff (`wp_blunt`), Hand to Hand: Martial Arts — `occSkillsCore` + `wpRules` + `handToHandRules` (pp. 65–66).

classAbilities.name: Psionic Program
classAbilities.description: Eight Sensitive powers at 1st level; +1 power from any category (including master) per level after 1st — `ispEngine` (pp. 64–65).

classAbilities.name: Inner Strength Points (I.S.P.)
classAbilities.description: M.E.+6D6 at 1st level; +3D6 per additional level (pp. 64–65).

classAbilities.name: Power Points (P.P.E.)
classAbilities.description: 1D6×10+P.E. at 1st level; +2D4 P.P.E. per level — add P.E. attribute at chargen; `race_millek` vitals base `1D6*10` (pp. 64–65).

classAbilities.name: Starting Allies
classAbilities.description: Low-level PCs do not start with astral entity servants/bodyguards; some NPC Millek have 1–2 (pp. 65–66).

name: Tarantuloid R.C.C. Skills
description: Shadow O.C.C. for `race_tarantuloid` — astral-native warriors, craftsmen, farmers, or sorcerer-priests (pp. 68–69). Choose Warrior/Craftsman/Farmer or Sorcerer-Priest at creation via `specializations[]`. Ten R.C.C. skills at +10%, five at +5%, 1D4 languages, six secondary — medieval technology limits. Hand to Hand as purchased. Innate psionics: Mind Block, Empathy, Presence Sense. I.S.P. M.E.+4D6, +1D6/level. Sorcerer-Priests only: six spells levels 1–4 at 1st, +1 spell/level (`ppeEngine`).
packageNotes: Shadow O.C.C. for `race_tarantuloid` (auto-mounted via forcedOccId). Optional astral R.C.C. — GM may allow PCs in astral-centered campaigns (pp. 68–69).
Choose **Warrior/Craftsman/Farmer** (`occ_tarantuloid_warrior`) or **Sorcerer-Priest** (`occ_tarantuloid_sorcerer_priest`) at creation — ADA Field Agent specialization pattern.
Warrior/Craftsman/Farmer XP: `nightbane_core_snakebird_mystic` (parent `progression.xpTableId`). Sorcerer-Priest XP: `nightbane_core_nightbane_guardian` — override at chargen per specialization.
Alignment: any; Sorcerer-Priests must be Principled or Aberrant (pp. 68–69).
Cannot materialize on the physical world — astral traveler; ectoplasmic webs, combat bonuses, and astral senses on `race_tarantuloid` (pp. 68–69).
R.C.C. skill picks: ten skills +10%, five skills +5%, 1D4 languages, six secondary — medieval technology (`occSkillsCore` / `secondarySkills`) (p. 69).
Innate psionics (all branches): Mind Block, Empathy, Presence Sense — `ispEngine.grantedAbilityIds`. I.S.P. M.E.+4D6, +1D6/level (p. 69).
Magic (`ppeEngine`) applies to Sorcerer-Priest branch only — six spells from levels 1–4 at 1st; one additional spell per level. Warrior/Craftsman/Farmer have no spellcasting.

leyLine.notes: Innate psionics inherit psionic_global_rules.json ley-line/nexus multipliers when applicable.

psychicApm.notes: Combat as per Hand to Hand purchased; psionic attacks per melee equal physical attacks when psionic powers are active.

occSkillsCore.label: Ten R.C.C. skills of choice (+10%) — medieval technology

specializations.name: Warrior / Craftsman / Farmer
specializations.description: Defenders, greysteel smiths, and moss-farmers — the most common Tarantuloid paths. No magical spellcasting. Uses Mystic/Snakebird XP table (`nightbane_core_snakebird_mystic`).

armor: Chain mail (A.R. 13, S.D.C. 90) or scale armor (A.R. 15, S.D.C. 100)
weapons: Heavy spear (2D8 + P.S. damage) or other archaic weapon (sword, axe, etc.)
miscellaneous: Greysteel-forged tools or personal gear as appropriate to role

classAbilities.name: No Spellcasting
classAbilities.description: Warriors, craftsmen, and farmers have no magical abilities — ignore parent `ppeEngine` at chargen (p. 69).

classAbilities.name: Experience Table
classAbilities.description: Uses Mystic/Snakebird experience table (`nightbane_core_snakebird_mystic`) — parent `progression.xpTableId` (pp. 68–69).

classAbilities.name: Typical NPC Level
classAbilities.description: Warriors and slavers average 1D6 level of experience (p. 69).

specializations.name: Sorcerer-Priest
specializations.description: Respected magicians who lead, judge, and advise Tarantuloid communities. Must be Principled or Aberrant. Intuitive spell knowledge — six spells levels 1–4 at 1st, +1 spell per level. Uses Nightbane/Guardian XP table (`nightbane_core_nightbane_guardian`).

classAbilities.name: Alignment Restriction
classAbilities.description: Sorcerer-Priests must be Principled or Aberrant alignment (p. 69).

classAbilities.name: Intuitive Initial Spell Knowledge
classAbilities.description: Six spells from levels 1–4 at 1st level — `ppeEngine.progressionRoadmap` level 1 (`roadmap_only`) (p. 69).

classAbilities.name: Intuitive Spell Advancement
classAbilities.description: One additional spell per level of experience, each from spell levels equal to or below current character level — `ppeEngine.progressionRoadmap` levels 2–15 (p. 69).

classAbilities.name: Mystic Magic Parity
classAbilities.description: Spell strength at levels 4/8/12 (`ppeEngine.spellStrengthProgression`); +1 save vs magic at levels 3/6/9/12 (`staticBonuses.levelGatedSaves`) — same pattern as Mystic O.C.C. (p. 69).

classAbilities.name: P.P.E. Reservoir
classAbilities.description: P.E.×5 at 1st level; +12 P.P.E. per additional level — `ppeEngine.baseFormula` / `perLevelFormula` (p. 69; P.P.E. varies with O.C.C.).

classAbilities.name: Experience Table
classAbilities.description: Uses Nightbane/Guardian experience table (`nightbane_core_nightbane_guardian`) — override parent `progression.xpTableId` at chargen (pp. 68–69).

classAbilities.name: Typical NPC Level
classAbilities.description: Sorcerer-priests average 1D6+2 level of experience (p. 69).

classAbilities.name: GM Approval Optional R.C.C.
classAbilities.description: Dominant astral race — player characters at GM discretion in astral-centered campaigns (pp. 68–69).

classAbilities.name: R.C.C. Skill Program
classAbilities.description: Ten skills +10%, five skills +5%, 1D4 languages, six secondary — medieval technology limits (`occSkillsCore`, `secondarySkills`) (p. 69).

classAbilities.name: Innate Psionics (All Branches)
classAbilities.description: Mind Block, Empathy, Presence Sense — `ispEngine.grantedAbilityIds` (p. 69).

classAbilities.name: Inner Strength Points (I.S.P.)
classAbilities.description: M.E.+4D6 at 1st level; +1D6 per additional level — `ispEngine` (p. 69).

classAbilities.name: Natural Combat & Astral Abilities
classAbilities.description: Ectoplasmic webs, +2 initiative, +2 parry, +2 pull punch, +3 save vs horror factor, +2 attacks per melee — `race_tarantuloid` innateBonuses and classAbilities (pp. 68–69).

classAbilities.name: Materialization Limits
classAbilities.description: Cannot materialize on the physical world — cross-ref `race_tarantuloid` (p. 69).

classAbilities.name: Alliances
classAbilities.description: May befriend Nightbanes and Earth champions against the Ba'al; Ba'al and minions are deadly enemies (p. 69).

name: Necrophim R.C.C. Skills
description: Shadow O.C.C. for `race_necrophim` — Dark Angels of the Astral Plane (pp. 74–77). Fixed lores and W.P.s plus eight elective skills at +5%. Hand to Hand: Martial Arts (included). Innate psionics and intuitive spell knowledge. I.S.P. M.E.+4D6, +1D6/level. P.P.E. 6D6+6+P.E., +3D6/level.
packageNotes: Shadow O.C.C. for `race_necrophim` (auto-mounted via forcedOccId). Optional player R.C.C. with explicit GM approval (pp. 75–76).
Alignment: Diabolic or Aberrant typical; renegades may be Anarchist, Unprincipled, or Scrupulous — no Principled Necrophim (pp. 75–76).
Reputation: Guardians, Nightbanes, humans (especially Spook Squad), and other 'good' supernaturals may attack on sight; other Necrophim hunt renegades (pp. 75–76).
Astral form, materialization, shape-shifting, Dreamstream, and multi-plane vitals on `race_necrophim` — see `classAbilities` cross-refs (pp. 75–76).
Magically understands and speaks all languages spoken in presence; reads a language after 4 hours' exposure to literate speakers — `classAbilities` (p. 77).

Spell program: two spells each from levels 1–4 at 1st (eight total); one new spell per level at or below experience level — `ppeEngine` (`roadmap_only`; cannot learn spells independently) (pp. 76–77).
Experience: `between_shadows_necrophim` XP table; PCs start at 1st level (p. 77).

leyLine.notes: Innate psionics inherit psionic_global_rules.json ley-line/nexus multipliers when applicable.

psychicApm.notes: Combat as Hand to Hand: Martial Arts; psionic attacks per melee equal physical attacks when psionic powers are active.

occSkillsCore.label: Eight other skills of choice (+5%) — physical, weapon, technical, or rogue; little technology/human society

miscellaneous: Wrist chains (2D6 damage + P.S.; entangle to 60 ft / 18.3 m)
Black tunic or bizarre leather body armor as preferred

classAbilities.name: GM Approval Optional R.C.C.
classAbilities.description: Dark Angels usable as player characters with Game Master approval — may serve powers more dangerous than the Nightlords (pp. 75–76).

classAbilities.name: Fixed R.C.C. Skill Package
classAbilities.description: Lore: Astral +15%, Lore: Dreamstream +10%, Lore: Nightlands +10%, W.P. Chain, W.P. Sword, plus eight electives at +5% — `occSkillsCore` + `wpRules` (pp. 76–77).

classAbilities.name: Universal Language Comprehension
classAbilities.description: Magically understands and speaks all languages spoken in presence; reads a language after 4 hours' exposure to literate speakers (p. 77).

classAbilities.name: Hand to Hand: Martial Arts
classAbilities.description: Martial Arts and entangle (via W.P. Chain) at 1st level — `handToHandRules.defaultSkillId: hth_martial_arts` (p. 76).

classAbilities.name: Innate Psionics
classAbilities.description: Mind Block, Presence Sense, See Aura, See the Invisible, Astral Navigation — `ispEngine.grantedAbilityIds` (p. 77).

classAbilities.name: Inner Strength Points (I.S.P.)
classAbilities.description: M.E.+4D6 at 1st level; +1D6 per additional level — `ispEngine` (p. 77).

classAbilities.name: Intuitive Initial Spell Knowledge
classAbilities.description: Two spells each from levels 1–4 at 1st level (eight total) — select via `ppeEngine` level-1 roadmap; two per spell level band (pp. 76–77).

classAbilities.name: Intuitive Spell Advancement
classAbilities.description: One new spell per level at or below current experience level — `ppeEngine.progressionRoadmap` levels 2–15 (`roadmap_only`; cannot learn or purchase spells independently) (pp. 76–77).

classAbilities.name: Power Points (P.P.E.)
classAbilities.description: 6D6+6+P.E. at 1st level; +3D6 P.P.E. per level — `ppeEngine` / `race_necrophim` vitals `averageStandardPpe` (add P.E. at chargen) (pp. 75–76).

classAbilities.name: Dreamstream Resistance
classAbilities.description: +2 vs dream manipulation at 1st; +1 at levels 4, 7, 10, and 13 — `staticBonuses.levelGatedSaves.save_dream_manipulation` (pp. 75–76).

classAbilities.name: Dreamdance Superior
classAbilities.description: Enter the Dreamstream as per Dreamdance: Superior — cross-ref `race_necrophim` classAbilities (pp. 75–76).

classAbilities.name: Renegade Alliances
classAbilities.description: Renegades may work with human psychics, mystics, Nightbanes, or occasional Guardians — tentative and suspicious (p. 77).

name: Astral Lord P.C.C.
description: Humans with an innate affinity for the Astral Plane — enter at will, navigate all three regions, and reshape ectoplasm into personal astral domains. Most awaken in childhood; the First Foray is deadly. Long residence in astral space slows aging; some lords are centuries old yet appear middle-aged. After Dark Day, many oppose Nightlords or exploit their gifts for greed or crime.
packageNotes: M.E. 13 or higher required (p. 52).

P.C.C. skills: Native language (98%), Lore: Astral (+20%), two Technical (+10%), one Pilot (+10%), two Domestic (+10%); Hand to Hand purchased from Related skills — Basic 1 slot, Expert 2, Martial Arts or Assassin 3 (evil only) (pp. 51–52).
Related: 10 at creation; +1 at levels 3, 6, 8, 10, and 13. Espionage: Wilderness Survival only. Medical: +5% on First Aid only (p. 52).
Secondary: five skills — no O.C.C. % bonus; same category limits as Related (p. 52).
P.P.E.: 3D4×10 + P.E. at 1st level; +2D6 per level — channel into astral domains or psionic advancement (pp. 51–52).
I.S.P.: 1D4×10 + M.E. at 1st level; +1D4 per level; may permanently convert P.P.E. to I.S.P. 1:1 (pp. 51–52).
Psionics: two Sensitive + two Physical at 1st; +1 Sensitive or Physical per level — `ispEngine` (p. 52).
Earth gear modest — typical possessions 3D6×100 dollars; starting cash 2D6×100 (pp. 51–52).
Background should explain skill picks — teens/early adults rarely have higher education; archaic characters avoid high-tech skills (p. 52).

creationSelectionPlan.label: 1st level — two Sensitive and two Physical psionics

perLevelSelection.label: One Sensitive or Physical psionic per level

miscellaneous: Earth possessions per background — typical total value 3D6×100 dollars
Astral-domain items created with invested P.P.E. (see Building an Astral Domain, p. 54+)

classAbilities.name: Astral Transference
classAbilities.description: Enter the Astral Plane at will — 1 minute (4 melee rounds) meditation, no I.S.P. cost. Body (not unconfigured gear) becomes astral equivalent with all Astral Transference psionic abilities. May coexist on Earth/Nightlands or enter fully; reverse takes 1 minute. Astral travel costs no I.S.P.; physical-world astral form drains 1 I.S.P. per 5 minutes until solid or fully astral — drained I.S.P. not recovered until then (pp. 51–52).

classAbilities.name: Astral Senses
classAbilities.description: Sense energy entities and astral travelers in physical or astral form; detect ley lines and nexuses within 1 mile (1.6 km). Perception roll to recognize Nightbane, Guardians, Necrophim, and other supernatural beings — Moderate difficulty, Challenging if dampened by Mind Block or similar. In the Astral Plane, astral navigation active at no I.S.P. cost (pp. 51–52).

classAbilities.name: Astral Travel
classAbilities.description: After 1 minute concentration, arrive at a known, unwarded destination in the Outer Layer, Inner Plane, or personal astral domain — 2 I.S.P. per use. +2 I.S.P. per passenger brought along (simultaneous crossing). Passengers become astral forms with S.D.C. equal to 2×(S.D.C.+H.P.); after 1 hour in the Plane each loses 1 I.S.P./hour until returned to domain or physical world (pp. 51–52).

classAbilities.name: Astral Domain
classAbilities.description: Permanently channel P.P.E. into ectoplasm to build a pocket universe on the Inner Plane or Outer Layer — size, defenses, and features per Building an Astral Domain (p. 54+). Multiple lords may collaborate; all contributors gain full access and immunity to defenses. Improve by channeling more P.P.E. as experience grows (pp. 51–52).

classAbilities.name: Astral Reconfiguration (Objects)
classAbilities.description: Permanently spend I.S.P. to make objects astrally sensitive — usable in astral form, portable into the Astral Plane, and able to affect astral beings on Earth/Nightlands. Cost: 1 I.S.P. per pound (0.45 kg) or fraction; bullets/batteries may be grouped; loaded gun or flashlight+batteries as one unit. Replenish ammo/batteries separately (p. 52).

classAbilities.name: Ectoplasm Armor
classAbilities.description: 15 I.S.P. activates skin-tight ectoplasm armor until destroyed or 10 minutes/level. Astral Plane: 100 S.D.C. + 20/level, any shape. Earth/Nightlands: 25 S.D.C. + 20/level — invisible except to psychics, children 12 and under, astral forms, and supernatural beings. No A.R.; must deplete S.D.C. to reach the body (p. 52).

classAbilities.name: Psionic Advancement Program
classAbilities.description: Two Sensitive + two Physical picks at 1st level; +1 Sensitive or Physical per level thereafter — `ispEngine`. Innate Astral Transference on `grantedAbilityIds` (p. 52).

classAbilities.name: Power Points (P.P.E.)
classAbilities.description: 3D4×10 + P.E. at 1st level; +2D6 per level — invest in astral domains or psionic growth. See `ppeEngine` (pp. 51–52).

classAbilities.name: Inner Strength Points (I.S.P.) & P.P.E. Conversion
classAbilities.description: 1D4×10 + M.E. at 1st level; +1D4 per level. May permanently convert P.P.E. to I.S.P. 1:1 (P.P.E. base reduced). New-level P.P.E. may also be converted. See `ispEngine` (pp. 51–52).

classAbilities.name: First Foray & Longevity
classAbilities.description: Most lords awaken in childhood; spontaneous astral projection precedes the deadly First Foray when the "fantasy world" proves real. Extended astral residence slows physical aging — centuries-old lords may appear middle-aged. Insanity and hermit domains are common maladies (pp. 50–51).

classAbilities.name: Gray Ghost Society & Role-Playing
classAbilities.description: Only organization to systematically study Astral Lords. Player and G.M. should develop domain, allies, rivals, and era of origin; domain safety nets must not dominate play — Nightlords, predators, and invasions can humble over-reliance (pp. 50–51).

name: Astral Mage O.C.C.
description: Sorcerers mystically linked to the Astral Plane — magic and innate psionics drawn from the Outer Layer's ectoplasmic energy. Many sense a living "Great Spirit" permeating the Plane. Domains tend to be smaller than psychic Astral Lords because spellcasting competes for P.P.E. After Dark Day they treat Nightlords as a universal threat and defend astral politics, Earth, and allied realms.
packageNotes: I.Q. 10 or higher; high M.E. desirable (p. 54).

O.C.C. skills: Native language (98%), Lore: Astral (+20%), two Technical (+10%), one Electrical or Mechanical (+10%), one Pilot (+10%), two Domestic (+10%); Hand to Hand from Related — Basic 1, Expert 2, Martial Arts or Assassin 3 (evil) (p. 54).
Related: 11 at creation; +1 at levels 3, 6, 8, 10, and 13. Technical +10% (not +5%). Espionage: any (p. 54).
Secondary: five skills — no O.C.C. % bonus; same category limits as Related (p. 54).
Spells: four levels 1–3 at 1st; two new spells per level (same or lower level) — `ppeEngine.progressionRoadmap` (pp. 53–54).
P.P.E.: 3D6×10 + P.E. at 1st; at least one-third (rounded up) must fund starting astral domain; +2D4 per level (pp. 53–54).
Psionics: Empathy, Mind Block, See the Invisible, Telepathy — `ispEngine.grantedAbilityIds` (p. 54).
I.S.P.: 4D6 + M.E. at 1st; +1D6 per level (p. 54).
Finances: disinterested in Earth — 1D6×100 or less; materially active — 1D4×1000 cash and 2D4×1000 possessions (G.M. adjusts) (p. 54).

occSkillsCore.label: One Electrical or Mechanical skill of choice

miscellaneous: Earth gear per lifestyle — G.M. sets fair baseline
Astral-domain features funded from mandatory one-third starting P.P.E. (see Building an Astral Domain, p. 54+)

finances.blackMarketAssets: 2D4*1000 in possessions — or 1D6*100 cash for characters with little Earth interest (p. 54)

classAbilities.name: Astral Transference
classAbilities.description: Same innate astral entry as Astral Lords — 1 minute meditation, no I.S.P.; astral form per Astral Transference power. Physical-world astral form drains 1 I.S.P. per 5 minutes until solid or fully astral (pp. 53–54).

classAbilities.name: Astral Senses
classAbilities.description: Sense astral travelers and energy entities; ley lines/nexuses within 1 mile (1.6 km). Perception roll to detect supernatural beings — Moderate, Challenging if mind-blocked. Free astral navigation in the Astral Plane (pp. 53–54).

classAbilities.name: Astral Travel
classAbilities.description: Known, unwarded destinations in Outer Layer, Inner Plane, or personal domain — 2 I.S.P.; +2 I.S.P. per passenger. Passenger astral S.D.C. and hourly I.S.P. drain rules as Astral Lord (pp. 53–54).

classAbilities.name: Astral Domain
classAbilities.description: Channel permanent P.P.E. into ectoplasm pocket realms — typically smaller than lord domains because spell P.P.E. competes with construction. Collaborators share full access. See Building an Astral Domain (p. 54+); at chargen invest at least one-third of rolled P.P.E. (rounded up) (pp. 53–54).

classAbilities.name: Magical Powers
classAbilities.description: Four wizard spells levels 1–3 at 1st level; two new spells per level (same or lower level) — `ppeEngine.progressionRoadmap` (pp. 53–54).

classAbilities.name: Power Points (P.P.E.)
classAbilities.description: 3D6×10 + P.E. at 1st level; minimum one-third (rounded up) funds starting domain; +2D4 per level. See `ppeEngine` (pp. 53–54).

classAbilities.name: Innate Psionics & I.S.P.
classAbilities.description: Empathy, Mind Block, See the Invisible, Telepathy at 1st level — `ispEngine.grantedAbilityIds`. I.S.P.: 4D6 + M.E.; +1D6 per level (p. 54).

classAbilities.name: Great Spirit & Astral Politics
classAbilities.description: Many mages sense a sentient presence — the "Great Spirit" — permeating the Plane. They defend all domains and regions from Nightlords, monsters, and intruders, often allying across species to protect astral and physical realities (pp. 52–53).

classAbilities.name: Nightlord War & Dark Day
classAbilities.description: After Dark Day, astral mages treat Ba'al as a universal threat to the connected astral and material worlds (p. 53).

name: Dream Maker P.C.C.
description: Psychics who reshape the Dreamstream — enter asleep or bodily from 2nd level, manipulate dreams, create dream objects, open rifts between worlds, and Dreamkill. Many abandon Earth for personal dream domains; madness is an occupational hazard. Since Dark Day, many oppose Nightlord invasion of the Dreamstream.
packageNotes: M.E. 14 or higher; humans only (p. 96).
Dream travel: Dreamdance (Minor) at 1st; Dreamdance (Superior) from 2nd level — innate, no I.S.P. (pp. 94–95).

O.C.C. skills: Native language (98%), Lore: Dreamstream (+25%), two Technical (+10%), two Domestic (+10%); Hand to Hand from Related — Basic 1 slot, Expert 2, Martial Arts or Assassin 3 (evil) (p. 96).
Related: 10 at creation; +1 at levels 3, 5, 8, 11, and 13. Domestic +10%. Medical: First Aid, Paramedic, or Holistic Medicine only (p. 96).
Secondary: five skills — no O.C.C. % bonus; same category limits as Related (p. 96).
Psionics: two Sensitive at 1st; +1 Sensitive at levels 3, 6, 9, and 12 — `ispEngine` (p. 95).
I.S.P.: M.E.×5 + 2D6 at 1st level; +2D6 per additional level (p. 96).
Insanity save each level from 2nd — see `classAbilities` (p. 96).
Dream domain I.S.P. upgrades — manual until `dream_domain_forge` ships; see `docs/forge/sub_forge_backlog.md` (p. 95).

miscellaneous: Modest Earth possessions — G.M. may allow up to 3D6×1,000 dollars in gear
Some favor garish vans or basic shelter/clothing/food (p. 96)

finances.blackMarketAssets: Up to 3D6*1000 in possessions per G.M. approval (p. 96)

classAbilities.name: Dream Travel
classAbilities.description: Dreamdance (Minor) at 1st — body stays on Earth; dream-self killed wakes the character. Dreamdance (Superior) from 2nd level — body and soul enter the Dreamstream; death there is real. Up to 4 hours/level per 24 hours (10 minutes spent per entry); indefinite from 6th level. No I.S.P. for P.C.C. dream travel (pp. 94–95).

classAbilities.name: Dream Manipulation
classAbilities.description: Reshape dream landscape or Dream Pool content — opposed D20 + dream manipulation bonuses vs dreamer's M.E. Horror factor up to 8 + level (max 18; +3 H.F. for creative descriptions). 10 I.S.P. per minute (p. 95).

classAbilities.name: Dream Attacks
classAbilities.description: Strike roll with dream manipulation bonuses; 4D6 damage at 1st + 1D6 per level. Normal dream "kills" wake the victim at H.F. 17; only Dreamkill harms the body. 10 I.S.P. per attack (pp. 94–95).

classAbilities.name: Dream Manipulation/Combat Bonuses
classAbilities.description: +2 at 1st; +1 at levels 2, 4, 7, 10, 13, and 15 (+ M.E.) — `staticBonuses.levelGatedSaves.save_dream_manipulation`. Self-heal 1D6 S.D.C. per 1 I.S.P. in the Dreamstream (one melee action) (pp. 94–95).

classAbilities.name: Create Dream Object
classAbilities.description: Manifest objects from dreamstuff — 1 cubic foot/level; S.D.C. min(3× real, 20/level); weapon damage max(real, 2D6/level); infinite ammo on guns. 12 I.S.P. per 2 minutes (p. 95).

classAbilities.name: Increase Attributes (Dreamstream)
classAbilities.description: Raise P.S./P.E. +1 per M.E.; P.P. +1 per 4 M.E.; S.D.C. +M.E.×3 — 5 I.S.P. per attribute (20 for all four). Duration 2 minutes/level (p. 95).

classAbilities.name: Open Window to the Dreamstream
classAbilities.description: 60 I.S.P. rift — passengers become dream entities; from a sleeper's Dream Pool may exit within 100 ft (30.5 m) of dreamer worldwide. Visible tear, 60 ft lights, psionic stun on lookers; cumulative 20%/round dream-creature egress (100% by round 5). Entities range 300 ft; 2 minutes/level duration (p. 95).

classAbilities.name: Dreamkill
classAbilities.description: Destroy dream persona S.D.C., then spend 60 I.S.P. + permanently sacrifice 2 I.S.P. — save vs psionics or −2D6 H.P. (death if exceeds P.E., else coma per Nightbane RPG p. 37) (p. 95).

classAbilities.name: Create Dream Domain
classAbilities.description: Strengthen personal Dream Pool — +15 save vs dream manipulation (5 permanent I.S.P.); dimensional portal 15 permanent I.S.P. (1-minute activation, or 1 melee for +10 permanent I.S.P.); movable portal +5 permanent I.S.P. Future UI: `dream_domain_forge` — `docs/forge/sub_forge_backlog.md` (p. 95).

classAbilities.name: Psionic Advancement Program
classAbilities.description: Two Sensitive picks at 1st; +1 Sensitive at levels 3, 6, 9, and 12 — `ispEngine` (p. 95).

classAbilities.name: Inner Strength Points (I.S.P.)
classAbilities.description: M.E.×5 + 2D6 at 1st level; +2D6 per level — `ispEngine` (p. 96).

classAbilities.name: Insanity: The Price of Power
classAbilities.description: From 2nd level, save vs insanity each new level on failure: 01–30% short-term (2D4 days), 31–60% long-term (3D4 weeks), 61–80% permanent, 81–90% fear of dream monsters (3D4 months; permanent after 3×), 91–00% fear of dreaming (1D6 days, lose dream powers and manipulation bonuses) (p. 96).

classAbilities.name: Mad Dreamers & Nightlord War
classAbilities.description: Hermits who lose reality may become homicidal Mad Dreamers. Active makers may spy, crusade, or assassinate on Earth; many now fight Ba'al in the Dreamstream (pp. 94–95).

name: Dream Dancer P.C.C.
description: Dreamstream explorers with Dreamdance travel, self-shaping, and strong resistance to dream manipulation — but without Dreamkill, dream domains, or rift windows. Wider psionic variety and lower insanity risk than Dream Makers; rivalry between the two groups is mostly social, not violent.
packageNotes: M.E. 12 or higher (p. 99).
Dream travel: Dreamdance (Minor) and (Superior) innate; indefinite duration; may exit at start point or within 100 ft (30.5 m) of a sleeper whose Dream Pool was visited (pp. 97–99).

O.C.C. skills: Native language (98%), Lore: Dreamstream (+20%), two Technical (+10%), two Domestic (+5%), one Rogue (+10%); Hand to Hand: Basic included — Expert 1 related slot, Martial Arts or Assassin 2 (evil) (p. 99).
Related: eight at creation; +1 at levels 3, 5, 8, 11, and 13 (p. 99).
Secondary: six skills — no O.C.C. % bonus; same category limits as Related (p. 99).
Psionics: one Sensitive, one Healing, one Physical at 1st; +1 from those categories per level — `ispEngine` (p. 99).
I.S.P.: M.E.×3 + 2D6 at 1st level; +2D6 per additional level (p. 99).
Cannot build dream domains, Dreamkill, or open Dreamstream windows (pp. 97–99).

creationSelectionPlan.label: One Sensitive, one Healing, and one Physical psionic at 1st level

perLevelSelection.label: One Physical, Sensitive, or Healing psionic per level

miscellaneous: Little beyond clothes on their back — 1D6×100 dollars in equipment typical (p. 99)

classAbilities.name: Dream Travel
classAbilities.description: Dreamdance (Minor) and (Superior) — indefinite stay; no I.S.P. P.C.C. cost. Exit at starting location or within 100 ft (30.5 m) of a sleeper whose Dream Pool was entered; random pools can land anywhere — know the sleeper and where they sleep (pp. 97–99).

classAbilities.name: Dream Manipulation (Self & Defense)
classAbilities.description: Lesser outward manipulation than Dream Makers; powers mostly self-directed. Resist and counter dream manipulation, spells, and supernatural effects targeting self only — not environment or others. Self-heal 1D6 S.D.C. per 1 I.S.P. (one melee action) (pp. 97–99).

classAbilities.name: Reshape Self
classAbilities.description: Change appearance in the Dreamstream — base 5 I.S.P. (P.B. 10); +1 P.B. per 1 I.S.P. (max 24); +1 H.F. per 1 I.S.P. (max 17). Mimic real person: +10 I.S.P. and 30% +5%/level (+I.Q.) skill roll (+5–15% if well known) (pp. 97–99).

classAbilities.name: Increase Attributes (Dreamstream)
classAbilities.description: Same formula as Dream Maker — P.S./P.E. +1/M.E., P.P. +1/4 M.E., S.D.C. +M.E.×3; 5 I.S.P. per attribute; 2 minutes/level (pp. 97–99).

classAbilities.name: Dream Combat Bonuses
classAbilities.description: +2 at 1st; +1 at levels 3, 6, 9, 12, and 15 (+ M.E.) — `staticBonuses.levelGatedSaves.save_dream_manipulation` (p. 99).

classAbilities.name: Psionic Advancement Program
classAbilities.description: One Sensitive, one Healing, one Physical at 1st; +1 from those three categories per level — `ispEngine` (p. 99).

classAbilities.name: Inner Strength Points (I.S.P.)
classAbilities.description: M.E.×3 + 2D6 at 1st level; +2D6 per level — `ispEngine` (p. 99).

classAbilities.name: Maker vs Dancer Rivalry
classAbilities.description: Outsiders confuse the classes; Makers look down on Dancers, Dancers see Makers as half-mad — rivalry is social, not usually violent. Order of Morpheus and Dream Riders include both (pp. 97–99).

classAbilities.name: Limitations vs Dream Maker
classAbilities.description: No dream domains, Dreamkill, or Dreamstream windows; reduced insanity risk from lighter reality-warping (pp. 97–99).
